metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the criterion arm of `eval_solver.run_case` (story 55b8) is actually landed on `main` before committing the preview endpoint design to it, OR explicitly add story 55b8 to this story's `Depends on` list and specify the exact `run_case` call signature (including `repo_root` param and `case` key) the preview will use.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Both halves now exist on main' and that the LLM preview reuses `eval_solver.run_case(prompt_id, {"input": <fixture>}, runner=…, repo_root=…)` — specifically 'the criterion arm added in story 55b8'. However, the actual `src/rebar/llm/eval_solver.py` in the working tree still contains only the original 3-id hardcoded dispatch (`completion-verifier` / `ticket-quality` / `spec-alignment`) with a bare `raise ValueError` for any other prompt_id. No criterion arm exists. The preview endpoint's LLM path depends on this arm to call `passes.pass1_chunk` over inline fixture text; without it the plan builds on an interface that does not yet exist.
findings.location: Preview entry point — reuse the LANDED runners (run 1 remediation section) / src/rebar/llm/eval_solver.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add story 55b8 to the `Depends on` list in the Scope section. In the preview dispatch description, replace the vague 'criterion arm added in story 55b8' with the concrete call the endpoint will make: `passes.pass1_chunk(runner, cfg, plan=fixture["input"], chunk=[registry.by_id(repo_root)[cid]], agentic=(registry.exec_tier(desc)=="AGENT"))` — this can be called directly without going through `run_case` if the arm hasn't landed, and documents the exact seam.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that a criterion-aware entry point exists (or define one to be created) that accepts an inline fixture + a criterion prompt_id and returns `{verdict, finding?, rationale}` — `eval_solver.run_case` as it stands does not provide this capability and the plan must either cite the correct existing function or explicitly scope the new code to be written.
findings.finding: `eval_solver.run_case` has no 'criterion arm': the actual function only dispatches on 3 hard-coded prompt IDs (`completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`) and raises `ValueError` for any other `prompt_id`. There is no generic criterion dispatch, no `{"input": <fixture>}` case path, and no `repo_root` keyword parameter. The plan's assertion that the preview reuses this function 'via the criterion arm added in story 55b8' is a verifiable capability gap — the named integration does not exist.
findings.location: Preview dispatch, entry point & atomic authoring — 'Preview entry point — reuse the LANDED runners' section; also AC: 'POST /criterion/preview dispatches … eval_solver.run_case criterion arm'
findings.suggested_fix: Name the new function explicitly — e.g. `eval_solver.run_criterion_preview(prompt_id, fixture_text, *, runner, repo_root)` — rather than claiming `run_case` already has a criterion arm. This makes the implementation scope concrete and avoids a false reuse claim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact LLM-criterion preview call path: either spec the new `eval_solver.run_case` criterion arm (call signature, how `fixture.input` maps to `pass1_chunk`'s `plan=` argument, which criterion descriptors are passed as the `chunk=` list) or replace the `eval_solver.run_case` reference with a direct `pass1_chunk` call and document the exact invocation.
findings.finding: The plan's LLM preview entry point — `eval_solver.run_case(prompt_id, {"input": <fixture>}, runner=…, repo_root=…)` with 'the criterion arm added in story 55b8' — does not exist in the codebase. `eval_solver.run_case` dispatches only on 'completion-verifier', 'ticket-quality', and 'spec-alignment'; any other prompt_id raises `ValueError`. There is no criterion arm in the landed code.
findings.location: Remediation run 1 — 'Preview entry point — reuse the LANDED runners' / AC: POST /criterion/preview dispatch
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'eval_solver.run_case(prompt_id, {"input": <fixture>}, runner=…, repo_root=…)' with the direct call: `passes.pass1_chunk(runner, cfg, plan=fixture["input"], chunk=[criterion_descriptor])` — this is what the criterion arm would do anyway, and it is the only landed path that takes inline plan text and a criterion chunk.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the async job+poll fallback API shape: specify the poll endpoint path (e.g. `GET /criterion/preview/{job_id}`), job-id format, and poll response schema (`{status: 'pending'|'done'|'error', result?: …}`) so the executor has a concrete contract to implement.
findings.finding: The async job+poll fallback shape — job-id format, poll endpoint path, and poll response schema — is described as something to 'define' but is never actually defined anywhere in the plan. An executor must invent this API from scratch, making the spike-gate fallback a placeholder deferring a real design decision.
findings.location: ## What — Spike gate bullet; ## Acceptance Criteria — spike-gate AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection under the spike-gate bullet: `GET /criterion/preview/{job_id}` returns `{status: "pending"|"done"|"error", result?: {verdict, finding?, rationale}, error?: str}`. Job IDs are opaque UUIDs. The editor UI polls at 2s intervals up to the configured timeout.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit test specifications for (a) the LLM criterion preview happy path (fixture triggers a finding → verdict='fire'; clean fixture → 'no-fire'), (b) the container/ISF 4xx, and (c) timeout behavior (what the endpoint returns and that the handler is not blocked).
findings.finding: The LLM criterion preview path — a new user-facing flow — has no explicit test specified anywhere in the plan. The DET path has a behavioral test ('planted violation fires / clean fixture does not'), but the equivalent for the LLM arm (`eval_solver.run_case` criterion arm returning verdict+finding) is entirely absent. Additionally, the failure paths for the new endpoint — timeout expiry (does it 503? trigger async?), the 4xx for container/ISF criteria, and a malformed/missing `criterion_id` request — have no test coverage specified.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions) / ## DET preview materializes the fixture (remediation, run 2)
findings.suggested_fix: For (a): add an AC 'a test submits a known-bad fixture against an LLM criterion and asserts verdict=fire with a non-empty finding; a clean fixture asserts verdict=no-fire.' For (b): 'a test submits a container-exec criterion to /criterion/preview and asserts HTTP 4xx with a human-readable error.' For (c): specify the timeout response code/body and assert it in a test with a mocked slow runner.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what new glue code bridges `passes.pass1_chunk` (or an alternative LLM call) to the `{verdict, finding?, rationale}` response contract — acknowledge the output-shape transformation rather than implying direct reuse.
findings.finding: `passes.pass1_chunk` is a batch rubric-chunk runner, not an inline fixture fire/no-fire probe. Its signature `(runner, cfg, *, plan: str, chunk: list[dict], agentic: bool)` takes a plan text + a rubric chunk and returns `list[dict]` shaped as plan-review findings (with `finding`, `criteria`, `evidence`, `scenarios`, `impact`, `checklist_item`, `suggested_fix` fields). The plan claims the LLM preview runs 'as its Pass-1 finder over the fixture text via `eval_solver.run_case(…)` (which wraps `passes.pass1_chunk`)' and returns `{verdict: "fire"|"no-fire", finding?, rationale}`. Neither the output shape nor the invocation contract matches; firing vs. non-firing on a fixture is not expressible as a `pass1_chunk` call without substantial new glue code the plan does not acknowledge.
findings.location: Preview dispatch, entry point & atomic authoring — 'Preview entry point — reuse the LANDED runners' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge and mitigate the coupling to `det_invariants._run_one` (a private symbol): either promote a public `run_one_criterion(criterion_id, fixture, repo_root)` function in det_invariants as the stable interface, or document the coupling risk and pin it as a known internal dependency.
findings.finding: The plan calls `det_invariants._run_one` (an underscore-prefixed internal) from the editor's preview endpoint without acknowledging or mitigating this coupling to a private implementation detail that may be refactored without notice.
findings.location: Preview entry point — reuse the LANDED runners / DET invariant path; 'the way `plan_review/det_invariants._run_one` does'
findings.suggested_fix: Extract a public function `det_invariants.run_preview(criterion_id, fixture_content, filename, fail_mode)` that the editor calls; this makes the contract explicit and decouples the editor from the internal implementation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decompose into at least two child tickets: (1) a spike/gate ticket resolving sync-vs-async (with the async job+poll interface defined and tested if chosen), and (2) the authoring form + preview endpoint implementation — so the spike outcome gates the implementation ticket before UI work begins.
findings.finding: The ticket is a single leaf but mixes UI (routing-fields form, preview UI in editor_assets/) with backend (new HTTP endpoint, DET materialization, atomic dual-write, async fallback), touches ≥3 files, ≥3 layers, introduces a new interface (POST /criterion/preview), and accumulates >6 distinct acceptance criteria across the original + two remediation rounds — yet has no formal decomposition into child tickets. The spike gate is described inline rather than as a gated child, and the conditional async job+poll fallback is an open deliverable that could expand scope mid-execution.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `inline` DET preview requires the detector file to pre-exist on disk, and add a distinct 4xx (e.g. `{error: 'detector_not_found', selector: ...}`) when the selector resolves to an empty Registry — mirroring the container/ISF 4xx already defined for other non-runnable cases.
findings.finding: The `inline(prompt+routing)` path for a DET criterion preview has a hidden ordering/mechanism gap: the `detector` selector in the inline routing still resolves against the on-disk detector registry (`.rebar/detectors/`). If the detector YAML file does not yet exist on disk when the user triggers an inline DET preview, `_matching_detectors()` returns an empty Registry and the preview returns `no_matching_detector → abstain`, silently appearing as 'no coverage' rather than a real must-fire probe. The plan does not specify whether inline DET preview requires the detector file to pre-exist, nor does it define a clear 4xx for this case (unlike the container/ISF 4xx it does define).
findings.location: ## Preview dispatch, entry point & atomic authoring — `POST /criterion/preview` dispatch section; also Remediation run 2 DET preview materialization AC
findings.suggested_fix: In the endpoint's DET arm, after `_matching_detectors()`, if the registry slice is empty: return a 4xx `{verdict: null, error: 'detector_not_found', detail: 'selector <selector> matched no detector files in .rebar/detectors/; create the detector YAML first'}` rather than an abstain response. This makes the inline DET preview authoring loop actionable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the auth/credentials preconditions for the LLM preview endpoint: which `agents`-extra classes or env vars must be present, and define the error response (e.g. `{"error": "agents extra not installed"}` 4xx) returned when `runner.preflight()` fails.
findings.finding: Auth/credentials preconditions for the live LLM runner inside the editor's HTTP handler are unstated. The plan says the LLM preview requires 'agents extra + credentials' but never specifies what API key / config is needed, where the editor's handler resolves a live `Runner` instance, or how a `runner.preflight()` failure (missing extra or missing API key) surfaces to the user instead of hanging or crashing the loopback handler.
findings.location: What — 'POST /criterion/preview endpoint … reusing the story #3 per-criterion eval runner behind the agents extra + credentials'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a precondition check at the top of the `POST /criterion/preview` handler: call `runner.preflight()` and, on `LLMConfigError`, return a `503` with `{"error": "LLM runner unavailable: <detail>"}` rather than letting the exception propagate to the loopback handler.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the filename-inference algorithm precisely: specify the selection rule when `det.languages` maps to multiple extensions (e.g., first extension in `_LANG_EXTENSIONS[lang]`), and specify what happens when `fixture.filename` is absent AND the detector declares no language or `file_globs` (the 'unconstrained' case — `preview.txt` will cause all language-scoped detectors to abstain).
findings.finding: The filename-inference decision rule for the DET preview temp file is underspecified when a detector's declared language maps to multiple extensions. The plan says default to 'a name the detector's `languages`/`file_globs` accept (e.g. `preview.py` for a Python-scoped rule)' but gives no rule for selecting among multiple extensions (e.g., 'javascript' maps to .js, .jsx, .mjs, .cjs in engine_b._LANG_EXTENSIONS). Since `engine_b._is_applicable` skips a detector as 'unsupported_lang' if the materialized directory contains no file with a matching extension, a wrong default silently produces an abstain instead of a scan.
findings.location: Remediation run 2 — 'DET preview materializes the fixture' / AC checklist item

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `rationale` value for the no-fire path in both the LLM arm (pass1_chunk returns []) and the DET arm (no match records) — e.g., a canned 'No findings for this criterion on the provided fixture' or the raw LLM response text if available.
findings.finding: The `rationale` field of the `POST /criterion/preview` response is undefined for the no-fire path in the LLM arm. The response contract specifies `{verdict: "fire"|"no-fire", finding?: <findings.py shape>, rationale: str}`, and `rationale` is non-optional. When `pass1_chunk` returns an empty findings list (verdict = no-fire), there is no finding text to quote. The plan never specifies what `rationale` contains in this case.
findings.location: What / POST /criterion/preview endpoint definition + Remediation run 1 response contract

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Protect the read-modify-write of criteria_routing.json with a process-level lock (e.g., `threading.Lock` held for the full read→merge→temp-write→os.replace sequence) so concurrent authoring requests under ThreadingHTTPServer cannot interleave and lose criterion entries.
findings.finding: The read-modify-write sequence on criteria_routing.json is not protected by a lock: under ThreadingHTTPServer, two concurrent authoring POSTs can both read the current JSON, both compute an updated version, and both os.replace — the last write wins and one criterion entry is silently dropped.
findings.location: Atomic dual-write for routing-fields authoring (remediation, run 1); 'temp-file + os.replace, mirroring prompt_authoring._atomic_write'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level `_overlay_lock = threading.Lock()` in the overlay-write helper and wrap the entire `json.load → merge → json.dump → os.replace` sequence in `with _overlay_lock:`.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Restate the spike-gate AC as an observable end-state: e.g. 'A committed `GET /criterion/preview/{job_id}` endpoint exists and a test demonstrates that a preview request exceeding the timeout returns a job-id response within 1s, with a subsequent poll returning the completed result.'
findings.finding: The spike-gate acceptance criterion is effort/process-framed ('The spike gate is resolved: a defined pass/fail threshold…') rather than stating a specific observable artifact or end-state that can be verified in-session. 'Resolved' is not a measurable outcome — it's a process milestone.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — 'The spike gate is resolved' AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or CLAUDE.md section recording the architectural decision: editor gains eval-runner capability via `POST /criterion/preview`, the exec-dispatch model (DET vs LLM), the sync-first/async-fallback tradeoff, and the spike-gate outcome — so future contributors have a decision record.
findings.finding: No ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc addition is planned for the novel architectural decision of giving the editor's HTTP handler live LLM/DET eval-runner capability — a structurally significant change the plan itself calls 'the one genuinely net-new piece.'
findings.location: Why section; Scope section; 'This is a structurally independent deliverable (its own endpoint + spike)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the UI error states for the routing-fields form: define what feedback is shown for invalid field values (client-side validation) and for a failed overlay write (server error → user-visible message), covering at least the 4xx/5xx response cases.
findings.finding: The routing-fields form's error states are unspecified: what the user sees when field values are invalid (e.g., bad exec value, non-numeric block_threshold) or when the atomic overlay write fails on the server is not described anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — routing-fields form authoring; 'Atomic dual-write for routing-fields authoring' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or prose section: 'The routing-fields form displays inline validation errors for each field (e.g., block_threshold must be 0–1) before submission; on a failed overlay write the editor shows a dismissible error banner with the failure reason and confirms the criterion is NOT active.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add coverage for (a) empty fixture submission (define the validation message or disabled-submit guard) and (b) the abandon/cancel exit from the criterion authoring form (e.g., unsaved-changes warning or explicit discard button).
findings.finding: The preview form's empty-fixture and abandon/cancel exit paths are not covered: what happens if the user submits a preview with no fixture text, and whether there is a cancel/discard path when authoring a criterion mid-form, are both unspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — preview AC; editor_assets/ (form + preview UI)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the UI behavior for the async fallback path: specify the loading/polling state shown to the user, a cancel affordance, and the UI response when the async job times out or errors (not just the server-side job+poll contract).
findings.finding: The async job+poll fallback path (triggered when sync preview exceeds the timeout) has no specified UI behavior: loading state during polling, how the user cancels an in-flight poll, and the exit path when the async job itself times out are all unspecified.
findings.location: 'Spike gate' section (What); Acceptance Criteria — spike gate AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add accessibility done-definitions to the `editor_assets/` form + preview UI scope: specify WCAG 2.1 AA compliance (keyboard navigation for all form controls, screen-reader labels on verdict/finding output, sufficient contrast for fire/no-fire indicators, and no color-alone signaling for verdict state).
findings.finding: The plan introduces new interactive UI in `editor_assets/` (a routing-fields form and a live preview result panel) but contains zero accessibility considerations — no WCAG 2.1 AA compliance language, no keyboard navigation spec, no screen-reader annotations, no contrast or touch-target requirements, and no reduced-motion handling.
findings.location: Scope — `editor_assets/` (form + preview UI); What — 'routing-fields form'; AC#1
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'The routing-fields form and preview result panel are keyboard-navigable (tab order, Enter/Space activation), all inputs and verdict output carry ARIA labels, fire/no-fire verdict is not signaled by color alone (icon or text accompanies color), and contrast ratios meet WCAG 2.1 AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI components).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
